# Pr 24930 Openai Resp V3 Seed Metadata Reassembly Compat

Source type: github
Document ID: dsid_6c58ddb8ecf543d89fca8352351dea29
Release: v1.0.0
Benchmark: EnterpriseRAG-Bench synthetic upstream data
openai-compat: responses-v3 deterministic-seed routing + metadata passthrough and streaming reassembly

Motivation: the OpenAI Responses API v3 introduces a seed/determinism flag and richer per-response metadata fields. Customers expecting parity through our OpenAI compatibility layer were hitting two classes of issues: 1) requests with deterministic seeds sometimes routed to fallback model variants causing non-deterministic behavior, and 2) streaming reassembly and function/tool-calling events lost user-supplied metadata (e.g., trace ids) on intermediate fragments. This PR: - Propagates the new 'seed' and 'determinism' fields from OpenAI-style Requests through our compatibility translation layer so upstream callers can control RNG determinism when we map to compatible model variants. - Adds per-request metadata passthrough: preserves and forwards top-level metadata and nested response-level metadata (user supplied) through streaming frames and the final assembled Response object. - Stabilizes streaming reassembly for mixed partials (text chunks, tool call fragments, structured JSON outputs) by introducing a sequence/frame id and last-frame markers in the compat layer; this avoids interleaving when a single call fan-outs to auxiliary model variants. - Ensures function/tool-calling events are emitted with the original 'metadata' and 'seed' fields intact so SDKs relying on tool traceability continue to work. - Adds exhaustive integration tests against the Responses v3 contract and extends streaming unit tests to emulate packet reorder and intermittent delivery. Implementation notes: - New compatibility translator: redwood/openai_compat/responses_v3_translator.py maps OpenAI fields to internal inference request flags (seed->rng_seed, determinism->sampling_profile). Translator is intentionally minimal: we preserve unknown fields in a passthrough map under compat_metadata for future extensions. - Streaming layer changes: redwood/api/streaming.py: implement frame_sequence_id and final_fragment bool. Reassembly uses (request_id, frame_sequence_id) ordering plus a small re-sequencing buffer gated by latency_slo to avoid head-of-line blocking. - Routing: routing/policy.py updated to prefer exact-architecture variants when determinism is requested; fallback still allowed if pinned variant unavailable. - Tests: tests/integration/test_responses_v3_compat.py includes deterministic-seed repeatability checks, metadata round-trip assertions, and simulated packet reordering scenarios. - Docs: docs/openai_compat.md updated with Responses v3 mapping and guidance for determinism semantics. Backwards compatibility: default behavior unchanged if seed/determinism not provided. We intentionally do not make determinism true by default because it impacts throughput. Checklist: - [x] Translator for seed/determinism fields - [x] Metadata passthrough in streaming frames - [x] Stable reassembly and ordering buffer - [x] Routing preference when determinism requested - [x] Integration + unit tests - [x] Docs update - [x] CI passing on rerun Rollout/rollback notes: feature gated behind a compatibility flag (OPENAI_COMPAT_V3=true) and an experiment toggle for routing. If customer issues are observed around tail latency with deterministic requests, the routing policy can be relaxed to fallback earlier while preserving seed values in logs for replay/diagnosis.
Amit Patel: The translator looks good overall. One open question: if the requested seed exceeds 64 bits, how should we normalize? Suggest we hash down with siphash and record the original in compat_metadata. Can you add a test for large seeds?
Marisol Vega: Responding to Amit: added normalization using siphash64 in translator and test case in tests/integration/test_responses_v3_compat.py line ~120. Original seed preserved in compat_metadata.seed_raw for auditability.
Claire Nguyen: Reassembly buffer hardens well but we should be conservative with the latency_slo default. Recommend 20ms default or tie it to per-customer SLO config. Also noted minor lint issues in streaming.py.
Marisol Vega: Reduced default re-sequence buffer to 20ms and moved config to per-customer tunables. Fixed lint comments and added comments describing tradeoffs.
Benji Rosen: Verified end-to-end locally including tool call round-trips; approved after Marisol pushed the siphash change and the docs clarification.
CI Bot: Build #2245: lint failed on first run; second run passed (all checks green).
